output:
I‚Äôm reviewing story `21-3-extract-workflow-machines` as a code review against the current workspace. I‚Äôll inspect the local diff, relevant story artifacts, and run targeted verification where needed before I give a pass/fail verdict.I‚Äôve confirmed the story is still local and uncommitted, with the implementation centered in `src/lib/workflow-machine.ts` and new `src/lib/workflow-machines.ts`. Next I‚Äôm reading the story artifact, proof, and code diff together to check for behavior drift and any mismatch between the claimed extraction and the actual public API.The proof claims all 13 ACs pass, including backward-compatible re-exports. I‚Äôm validating that claim against the actual source now, because this kind of extraction often leaves the build green while silently narrowing the public API.I‚Äôve read the extracted module. The main thing I‚Äôm testing now is whether the public surface promised in the story still exists from `workflow-machine.ts`, not just whether internal consumers compile.The code itself confirms the proof is overstating the export surface: `workflow-machine.ts` only re-exports `executeLoopBlock` and `dispatchTask`. I‚Äôm checking whether that mismatch is currently masked by limited test coverage or whether TypeScript catches any direct consumer breakage.The existing tests import the wrapper entry points and helper functions, but I‚Äôm not seeing any consumer that exercises the promised `runMachine`/`RunMachineContext` compatibility path. That means AC 13 can be false while the suite still passes, which is exactly the kind of gap this review needs to call out.I found a second API mismatch in the new module itself: `workflow-machines.ts` documents `loopMachine` and `epicMachine` as public exports, but both are still file-local `const`s. I‚Äôm pulling exact line references so the review can distinguish the wrapper-compat regression from the new-module export regression.1. High: backward-compatible exports from `workflow-machine.ts` were not preserved, so AC 13 is not actually satisfied. The story explicitly requires re-exporting `runMachine` and `RunMachineContext` from the legacy entrypoint, but [`workflow-machine.ts:27`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/src\/lib\/workflow-machine.ts#L27) only imports `runMachine` for local use and [`workflow-machine.ts:28`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/src\/lib\/workflow-machine.ts#L28) re-exports only `executeLoopBlock` and `dispatchTask`; [`workflow-machine.ts:29`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/src\/lib\/workflow-machine.ts#L29) imports `RunMachineContext` but does not re-export it. That directly contradicts the story task list at [`21-3-extract-workflow-machines.md:82`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/_bmad-output\/implementation-artifacts\/21-3-extract-workflow-machines.md#L82) and the proof‚Äôs AC 13 claim at [`21-3-extract-workflow-machines-proof.md:115`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/verification\/21-3-extract-workflow-machines-proof.md#L115). Build/tests passing does not prove this compatibility path because no consumer currently imports those symbols from `workflow-machine.ts`.

2. Medium: the new `workflow-machines.ts` module does not export the full machine API it documents, so the extraction is incomplete relative to its own contract. [`workflow-machines.ts:100`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/src\/lib\/workflow-machines.ts#L100) defines `loopMachine` as a private `const`, and [`workflow-machines.ts:321`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/src\/lib\/workflow-machines.ts#L321) does the same for `epicMachine`, even though the story requires those to be public at [`21-3-extract-workflow-machines.md:71`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/_bmad-output\/implementation-artifacts\/21-3-extract-workflow-machines.md#L71) and the subsystem docs advertise them as exports at [`AGENTS.md:89`](\/Users\/ivintik\/dev\/personal\/codeharness\/src\/lib\/AGENTS.md#L89). This is an API/documentation mismatch and an AC coverage gap for the extraction scope.

No concrete security defect stood out in the changed runtime logic; the failures are API-compatibility and acceptance-coverage regressions.

<verdict>fail</verdict>

<issues>
1. `workflow-machine.ts` no longer preserves the promised backward-compatible exports for `runMachine` and `RunMachineContext`, so AC 13 is unmet.
2. `workflow-machines.ts` does not export `loopMachine` or `epicMachine` despite the story and docs claiming those are part of the new module‚Äôs public API.
</issues>
